title: Preparation of 1M  magnesium sulfate solution (MgSO4)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1vnjo","text":"Original source of the protocol: WormBook Methods http://www.wormbook.org/chapters/www_strainmaintain/strainmaintain.html","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":71}],"data":{}},{"key":"732gh","text":"This protocol is for making 1M magnesium sulfate solution, which is used in the preparation of worm media, like NGM (Nematode Growth Medium). materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"83lsp","text":"Magnesium sulfate:   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":19,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"del0l","text":"Filter: Rapid-Flow Nalgene 0.2µm aPES membrane, ref: 564-0020","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fa66p","text":"Vaccum pump: Fisher Brand, ref: FB70155","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":956573,"guid":"4372CC97341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Magnesium Suflate Heptahydrate","mol_weight":0,"sku":"M5921-500G","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Weigh 12.3 g, ±0.1 of magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO 4 ·7H 2 O), and add it to a 100 mL measuring cylinder.
Add a clean stirring magnet, and leave it on a stirrer until all crystals are dissolved.
Remove the stirring magnet: To prevent touching the solution, use another magnet from the outside of the measuring cylinder to slide the stirring magnet out of the cylinder.
Fill up to 50 mL with milliQ water
Store at 4°C. We usually use it without problem for up to at least a year after preparation.
creator: APE Lab
